meaning_english: Meaning: Brisk and cheerful readiness. Opposite: Apathy, Lethargy, Reluctance, Slowness
Meaning: Showing a disinterested and selfless concern for the well-being of others. Opposite: Selfish, Egoistic, Greedy, Mean
Meaning: Open to more than one interpretation; not having one obvious meaning. Opposite: Clear, Precise, Explicit, Obvious
Meaning: Having or displaying a friendly and pleasant manner. Opposite: Hostile, Unfriendly, Grumpy, Rude
Meaning: Something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected. Opposite: Regularity, Standard, Normality, Conformity
Meaning: Enthusiastic or passionate. Opposite: Indifferent, Apathetic, Half-hearted, Cool
Meaning: Showing great care and perseverance. Opposite: Lazy, Negligent, Idle, Careless
Meaning: Extreme greed for wealth or material gain. Opposite: Generosity, Philanthropy, Charity
Meaning: So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring. Opposite: Original, Unique, Fresh, Innovative
Meaning: Hostile and aggressive. Opposite: Peaceful, Friendly, Amicable, Conciliatory
Meaning: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior. Opposite: Stable, Consistent, Steady, Reliable
Meaning: Simple or restrained; morally pure. Opposite: Corrupt, Immoral, Promiscuous, Impure
Meaning: Kept secret or done secretively. Opposite: Open, Public, Overt, Transparent
Meaning: Showing smug or uncritical satisfaction with oneself. Opposite: Concerned, Anxious, Humble, Dissatisfied
Meaning: Standing out so as to be clearly visible. Opposite: Hidden, Inconspicuous, Vague, Obscure
Meaning: Having normal physical functions suspended or slowed down for a period. Opposite: Active, Alert, Awake, Lively
Meaning: Cheerful and full of energy. Opposite: Depressed, Dull, Lifeless, Unenthusiastic
Meaning: Make (someone) ecstatically happy. Opposite: Miserable, Sad, Dejected, Gloomy
Meaning: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing. Opposite: Inarticulate, Silent, Mumbling, Hesitant
Meaning: Match or surpass (a person or achievement), typically by imitation. Opposite: Neglect, Ignore, Differ, Reject
Meaning: A person or thing that is mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand. Opposite: Solution, Clarity, Explanation, Obvious
Meaning: Having or showing great knowledge or learning. Opposite: Ignorant, Uneducated, Illiterate, Simple
Meaning: Make (a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling) worse. Opposite: Alleviate, Mitigate, Improve, Soothe
Meaning: Hold (someone or something) in very high regard; think or speak very highly of. Opposite: Humiliate, Condemn, Criticize, Lower
Meaning: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail. Opposite: Careless, Slovenly, Easy-going, Indifferent
Meaning: Sparing or economical with regard to money or food. Opposite: Extravagant, Wasteful, Lavish, Prodigal
Meaning: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. Opposite: Taciturn, Reticent, Silent, Concise
Meaning: Arrogantly superior and disdainful. Opposite: Humble, Modest, Meek, Submissive
Meaning: Perfectly clean, neat, or tidy. Opposite: Dirty, Filthy, Messy, Stained
Meaning: Not showing due respect for another person. Opposite: Polite, Respectful, Courteous, Modest
Meaning: Encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behavior). Opposite: Restrain, Discourage, Calm, Pacify
Meaning: Wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy. Opposite: Diligent, Active, Energetic, Industrious
Meaning: Not harmful or offensive. Opposite: Harmful, Toxic, Malignant, Dangerous
Meaning: Lacking flavor; lacking vigor or interest. Opposite: Tasty, Spicy, Exciting, Spirited
Meaning: Cheerful and friendly. Opposite: Gloomy, Morose, Miserable, Sad
Meaning: A passionate expression of grief or sorrow. Opposite: Celebrate, Rejoice, Exult, Cheer
Meaning: Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic. Opposite: Energetic, Vital, Active, Lively
Meaning: Make less severe, serious, or painful. Opposite: Aggravate, Worsen, Intensify, Enhance
Meaning: Sullen and ill-tempered. Opposite: Cheerful, Happy, Bright, Optimistic
Meaning: Wicked or criminal. Opposite: Virtuous, Noble, Good, Saintly
Meaning: Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion. Opposite: Flexible, Pliant, Compliant, Submissive
Meaning: Engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought. Opposite: Thoughtless, Shallow, Carefree, Flippant
Meaning: Not easily upset or excited; calm. Opposite: Agitated, Turbulent, Violent, Disturbed
Meaning: Spending money or resources freely and recklessly. Opposite: Frugal, Thrifty, Parsimonious, Miserly
Meaning: Complaining in a petulant or whining manner. Opposite: Content, Happy, Patient, Easy-going
Meaning: Having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment. Opposite: Foolish, Stupid, Ignorant, Unwise
Meaning: A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important to gain advantage. Opposite: Critic, Detractor, Honest, Rebel
Meaning: Tending to keep a firm hold of something; clinging or adhering closely. Opposite: Irresolute, Weak, Surrendering, Yielding
Meaning: Regard with great respect; revere. Opposite: Despise, Dishonor, Contemn, Scorn
Meaning: The time at which something is most powerful or successful. Opposite: Nadir, Bottom, Base, Floor
